findings.message: New state-mutating endpoint `archive_item` is untraced/unmetered beyond the generic ObservabilityMiddleware. There is no structured log entry recording the archive action (no correlation id, no item_id, no actor/outcome), no domain-specific metric (e.g. archive attempts/successes counter), and no explicit trace span for the DB mutation. A write-path that changes persistent state must emit a structured log with the correlation id and a counter so the operation is observable in Loki/Prometheus/Tempo.
findings.suggestion: Inject `get_logger()` and log `log.info("item_archived", item_id=item_id)` (binding the request correlation id) before/after the commit; add a domain counter (e.g. `recoverability`-style or a new app counter for archive_total/archive_failures_total) and consider an explicit OTel span around `session.commit()` so the DB write shows up in Tempo.

findings.message: The 404 error path (`item is None`) raises `HTTPException` with no log line. Error paths must be logged with the correlation id so operators can correlate a 4xx in the access log with a structured event; currently a missing-item lookup leaves no trace beyond the request middleware counters. (Also, `from fastapi import HTTPException` is imported inside the function body — top-of-module import keeps the error path predictable and avoids hiding it from static analysis.)
findings.suggestion: Before raising, log a warning with the correlation id: `log.warning("archive_item_not_found", item_id=item_id)`. Move the `HTTPException` import to the module top.

findings.message: No SLO threshold covers this new write endpoint's behaviour. The default SLOs in `observability/slo.py` are global (p99 latency + 5xx rate) and conflate reads and writes; a state-mutating archive endpoint should either be covered by a write-path-specific SLO (e.g. archive latency / archive error rate) or be explicitly excluded with a rationale. As-is, a regression in archive latency or 5xx rate is only visible diluted into the aggregate `app_request_*` counters.
findings.suggestion: Either add a write-path SLO (e.g. `archive_latency_p99_ms`) to `default_slos()` with PromQL mapped in `SLO_PROMQL`, or label requests by method/route in the metrics registry so `error_rate_pct` / `request_latency_p99_ms` can be sliced per endpoint.

findings.message: `session.commit()` has no surrounding error handling or trace span. If the commit fails (constraint violation, DB outage) the exception will bubble to the global handler — which will increment `app_unhandled_exceptions_total` but without any endpoint-specific context (no log line tying the failure to `item_id`, no break in the write attempt counter). The DB write is the riskiest line in the handler and currently the least observable.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the mutation+commit in a try/except, log a structured error with the correlation id and `item_id` on failure (then re-raise), and consider an explicit `tracer.start_as_current_span("archive_item.commit")` so the DB write is a distinct span in Tempo.
